_meta.verificationNote: AI Generated: Risk treatment options and strategies for customer information risks. Organisations should select treatment approaches based on their board-approved risk appetite and proportionality considerations.

title: Customer Information Risk Treatment Options
description: Framework for selecting and implementing risk treatment strategies for customer information risks under BNM MCIPD.

treatmentStrategies.label: Risk Avoidance
treatmentStrategies.description: Eliminate the risk by ceasing the activity or removing the source of risk entirely.
treatmentStrategies.applicability: Where the risk exposure exceeds the FSP's risk appetite and no cost-effective controls can reduce it to acceptable levels.
examples: Discontinuing a product or service that requires excessive customer data handling with insufficient safeguards
Ceasing cross-border data sharing with jurisdictions lacking adequate data protection frameworks
Eliminating the use of personal portable storage devices for customer data entirely
considerations: May limit business operations or revenue-generating activities
Should be a last resort when other treatment options are insufficient
Requires Board-level decision for material business impact

treatmentStrategies.label: Risk Mitigation
treatmentStrategies.description: Reduce the likelihood or impact of the risk through implementing or strengthening controls.
treatmentStrategies.applicability: The primary treatment strategy for most customer information risks, aligned with MCIPD control environment requirements.

subStrategies.label: Reduce Likelihood
subStrategies.description: Implement preventive controls to reduce the probability of the risk materialising.

treatmentOptions.name: Strengthen Access Controls
treatmentOptions.description: Implement or enhance role-based access controls, multi-factor authentication, and periodic access reviews to reduce the likelihood of unauthorised access.

treatmentOptions.name: Deploy Data Loss Prevention
treatmentOptions.description: Implement technical DLP controls across endpoints, email, and web channels to prevent unauthorised exfiltration of customer information.

treatmentOptions.name: Enhance Staff Training and Awareness
treatmentOptions.description: Strengthen training programmes to reduce human error and negligent disclosure, including phishing simulations and role-specific training.

treatmentOptions.name: Strengthen OSP Due Diligence and Monitoring
treatmentOptions.description: Enhance pre-engagement due diligence and ongoing monitoring of outsourced service providers to reduce the likelihood of OSP-related breaches.

treatmentOptions.name: Implement Physical Deterrence Programme
treatmentOptions.description: Deploy physical deterrence measures including CCTV, personal device restrictions, privacy screens, and clear-desk enforcement to reduce deliberate data capture attempts.

treatmentOptions.name: Encrypt Portable Devices and Data-in-Transit
treatmentOptions.description: Apply full-disk encryption on all portable devices and enforce encrypted transmission channels to reduce the likelihood of data compromise from lost or stolen devices.

subStrategies.label: Reduce Impact
subStrategies.description: Implement detective and corrective controls to limit the impact when a risk materialises.

treatmentOptions.name: Establish Breach Response Capability
treatmentOptions.description: Develop and test comprehensive breach response plans with escalation procedures, BNM notification templates, and customer notification protocols to minimise impact when breaches occur.

treatmentOptions.name: Deploy Anomaly Detection and Early Warning
treatmentOptions.description: Implement UEBA, SIEM monitoring, and automated alerting to detect breaches early and limit the volume of affected data and customers.

treatmentOptions.name: Implement Data Segmentation and Minimisation
treatmentOptions.description: Segment customer data stores and apply data minimisation principles to limit the blast radius of any single breach event.

treatmentOptions.name: Maintain Breach Register and Lessons Learnt Programme
treatmentOptions.description: Systematically capture breach data, root causes, and lessons learnt to continuously improve controls and prevent recurrence.

treatmentStrategies.label: Risk Transfer
treatmentStrategies.description: Transfer some or all of the financial impact of the risk to a third party, typically through insurance or contractual arrangements.
treatmentStrategies.applicability: Suitable for residual financial impact that remains after control implementation, but does not transfer regulatory accountability.
examples: Cyber insurance covering customer data breach costs including notification, forensics, and legal fees
Contractual indemnities from OSPs for breaches caused by their negligence or control failures
Professional indemnity insurance for directors and officers covering regulatory investigations
considerations: FSP retains full regulatory accountability regardless of insurance or contractual transfers
Insurance may not cover regulatory fines or penalties
Contractual indemnities are only as strong as the counterparty's financial capacity
Board must understand that risk transfer does not absolve governance obligations under MCIPD

treatmentOptions.name: Cyber Liability Insurance
treatmentOptions.description: Procure cyber insurance covering breach response costs, customer notification, forensic investigation, regulatory defence, and business interruption arising from customer information breaches.

treatmentOptions.name: OSP Contractual Indemnity
treatmentOptions.description: Include contractual indemnity provisions in OSP SLAs requiring the OSP to bear the financial costs of breaches caused by their negligence or failure to meet contractual obligations.

treatmentStrategies.label: Risk Acceptance
treatmentStrategies.description: Formally accept the risk when it falls within the FSP's risk appetite and the cost of further mitigation exceeds the potential benefit.
treatmentStrategies.applicability: Only for risks rated Low after control implementation, or where proportionality considerations under clause 10.4 justify acceptance.
requirements: Formal risk acceptance must be documented and approved by the designated officer under paragraph 9.2
Material risk acceptances must be reported to the Board under paragraph 8.3
Accepted risks must be periodically reviewed to ensure they remain within appetite
Risk acceptance does not exempt the FSP from compliance with MCIPD mandatory requirements (S-marked clauses)

treatmentOptions.name: Formal Risk Acceptance with Monitoring
treatmentOptions.description: Document and approve risk acceptance with ongoing monitoring through the regular risk review cycle, including trigger conditions for re-assessment.
applicableRisks: Risks rated Low after control implementation

treatmentSelectionCriteria.description: Criteria for selecting appropriate treatment strategies based on risk characteristics and organisational context.

factors.guidance: Critical and High risks: Mitigate as priority. Medium risks: Mitigate within 90 days. Low risks: Consider acceptance with monitoring.
Standard (S-marked) clause requirements must be complied with regardless of risk rating. Risk acceptance cannot override regulatory mandates.
Treatment intensity should be proportionate to the FSP's size, complexity, and volume/sensitivity of customer information per clause 10.4.
Evaluate the cost of controls against the potential financial and reputational impact of the risk materialising.
Consider technical feasibility, resource availability, and implementation timeline when selecting treatment options.
Prefer defence-in-depth with multiple control layers (policy, process, technical, physical) rather than relying on a single control type.

treatmentPlanTemplate.description: Standard template for documenting risk treatment plans aligned with MCIPD mitigation plan requirements (clause 10.2).

fields.description: Reference to the risk register entry
Specific treatment options selected from the catalogue
Controls from the library to be implemented or enhanced
Accountable person for implementing the treatment
Expected residual risk rating after treatment implementation
Who approved the treatment plan (designated officer or Board)
